prompt: The greenhouse cron host got rebuilt and whoever set it up runs every zsh job with warn_create_global and no_unset switched on — a decision I actually agree with, except our little overnight frost report now falls over. Run the normal way it's fine; run the way cron runs it, it dies before printing a single bed the moment FROST_LIMIT isn't exported (which it usually isn't, 0 is the default we document), it also dies when you don't pass the optional bed argument, and even on invocations that limp through, stderr fills up with 'created globally in function' chatter that the cron mailer dutifully forwards to the whole team every morning. `zsh test_frostwatch.zsh` is the gate: it checks the script still parses, then pins the report byte-for-byte under the strict options (with and without FROST_LIMIT and the bed filter) and demands a completely silent stderr — and then checks it once more without the options, because the report must come out identical both ways. Tidy the script up so all of that passes — and properly: the harness greps the script for setopt/unsetopt/emulate, so switching the host's options back off from inside is not a fix (ops would revert it on sight anyway). The report wording, bed ordering, and exit codes are what the growers' dashboard scrapes, so don't change any of it, and leave test_frostwatch.zsh alone.
notes: 5W5 item 6: zsh has no shellcheck, so this is the warning-adjacent seed — zsh -n plus a harness that runs the script under `zsh -o warn_create_global -o no_unset` and asserts byte-empty stderr, pinned stdout, and pinned exit codes (0 clear / 1 frosty / 2 usage), then re-runs plain to pin behavior parity. Planted defects, all probed on this box (zsh 5.9): bed_filter=$2 dies under no_unset when the optional arg is absent ('2: parameter not set'); limit=${FROST_LIMIT} dies when the env var is unset AND prints '(limit C)' instead of '(limit 0C)' on plain runs (night D pins the :-0 default, so exporting the var in the harness env is defeated by `unset FROST_LIMIT` + parity check); and warn_create_global fires for line/bed/temp in load_readings and limit/frosty/checked/bed in report (probed: plain assignment and for-loop variables in functions both warn; += on the typeset -g'd order array does not). Reference fix = ${2:-}, ${FROST_LIMIT:-0}, and local declarations — explicit typeset scoping per catalog. Directive-suppression loophole closed with a grep gate (precedent: 5E3 #4's eval ban): the harness rejects any setopt/unsetopt/emulate inside frostwatch.zsh, since an in-script unsetopt would silence the warnings without any scoping cleanup; the prompt announces the gate so it is fair. Even without the gate, nights A/D pin '(limit 0C)' so pure suppression cannot pass. Domain: nursery-bed frost monitoring — py-battcurve/py-sensorgrid are Python analytics; this is zsh option-hygiene cleanup on a cron reporter, different layer. Toolchain observed: zsh 5.9.
